title: [lichen_l2.c:1308] SECURITY: link_ctx initialized without Ed25519 keypair - TX unsigned
description:
Security finding: lichen_link_init() is called but lichen_link_load_key()/lichen_link_generate_key() is never called anywhere in bridge-zephyr.

Attack scenario: TX frames are sent without Schnorr-48 signatures because ctx->has_key == false. This is documented (line 827-828: 'signature if has_key') but never addressed. Receivers expecting signed frames will reject these.

Impact: Currently benign since RX also has no peer keys provisioned, but creates an insecure default configuration where:
1. TX has no authentication (no signature)
2. Anyone can forge frames claiming to be this node

This is noted in comments (line 280: 'peer_pubkey=NULL', line 1543: 'Once EDHOC handshake completes...') but EDHOC is not implemented.

Mitigation: Either:
1. Add a provisioning API to load keypairs (preferred for security)
2. Call lichen_link_generate_key() during init (temporary self-signed mode)
3. Document this as a known limitation with BUILD_ASSERT or LOG_WRN at startup
close_reason: Architecture: requires key provisioning system not yet implemented. TX signatures are intentionally omitted when has_key==false; this is documented behavior pending EDHOC implementation.
